Hook misuse: methods, bad returns, unknown phase, missing phase,
non-leading context.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import "context"

type T struct{}

//fabrik:hook setup
func (t *T) Setup() {}

//fabrik:hook setup
func Bad() int { return 0 }

//fabrik:hook verbose
func UnknownPhase() {}

//fabrik:hook
func MissingPhase() {}

//fabrik:hook setup
func TrailingCtx(name string, ctx context.Context) {}
-- want/diags --
error: web/web.go:7:1: //fabrik:hook must be on a package-level function (func Setup is a method)
  help: move the hook out of the method set
error: web/web.go:10:1: hook Bad must return nothing or error
  help: example: func MigrateDB(ctx context.Context) error
error: web/web.go:13:15: unknown lifecycle phase "verbose"
  help: the hookable phase is setup (after config, before providers); pre-intake and runtime work belong to commands
error: web/web.go:16:1: //fabrik:hook requires PHASE
  help: example: //fabrik:hook setup
error: web/web.go:20:31: hook TrailingCtx takes context.Context after other parameters
  help: a hook's context must be the first parameter
